O-01.name: Supply chain concentration & disruption

supplier_recovery_runway_weeks.source: erp_supply_chain.csv — TSMC critical-path supplier (18-week lead time)
supplier_recovery_runway_weeks.description: Inventory cover weeks for the critical-path single-source supplier (TSMC, 18-week lead time). Amber threshold 8w = TSMC target_inventory_weeks from ERP. Breach threshold 4w = minimum safe operating level (~22% of TSMC lead time). Current 5.2w = amber: 2.8 weeks below target, 12.8 weeks below TSMC lead time — a TSMC disruption today results in production stop in 5.2 weeks with no substitution path.
supplier_recovery_runway_weeks.threshold_rationale: TSMC 18-week lead time benchmark: amber at target inventory (8w), breach at 22% of lead time (4w)

O-01.agent_findings:
OPERATIONAL RISK ASSESSMENT — 2026-05-28

O-01 SUPPLY CHAIN
Single-source concentration stands at 62.6% of $12,610M total spend, materially above the breach threshold of 50%. Three suppliers are sole-sourced: TSMC ($4,200M, Semiconductors-CPU, Taiwan), Foxconn ($2,100M, Manufacturing-Assembly, China), and Quanta ($1,600M, ODM-Laptops, Taiwan). Together these three account for $7,900M of spend with no alternative qualified source.

The most acute near-term risk is Quanta [SUP005]. Its financial_health score of 61 is below the distress threshold of 65, its inventory cover of 3.9 weeks is below the breach threshold of 4 weeks, and its lead time is 6 weeks — meaning a supply failure today cannot be bridged by existing stock. This configuration simultaneously satisfies the escalation trigger (single-source supplier with financial_health < 65 AND inventory < 4 weeks). Quanta's OTD of 88.7% and rejection rate of 1.2% — the latter exceeding the O-03 breach threshold of 1.0% — indicate operational strain that may reflect its financial condition.

Yangtze Memory [SUP008], a multi-source supplier, carries a financial_health score of 55 — the weakest in the portfolio. While not single-sourced, its distress is relevant given China's 21.8% geographic concentration and the broader Taiwan-China-South Korea concentration of 96.6% of spend.

TSMC carries the largest single spend ($4,200M) with an 18-week lead time. An 18-week recovery window in a Taiwan disruption scenario — given Taiwan's 53.5% geographic concentration — is commercially catastrophic. The stress model quantifies revenue at risk at $8.2B and VaR at $2,373M at the 95th percentile. Recovery is modelled at 5 months. Dual-sourcing remediation is modelled to save $343M; inventory buffer build saves a further $275M in disruption cost. A demand shock probability of 24% with a 15% impact adds further uncertainty to the planning horizon.

Business continuity verdict: The organisation cannot sustain production beyond 3.9 weeks if Quanta fails, and cannot recover TSMC supply for approximately 18 weeks — 13 weeks beyond current TSMC inventory cover of 5.2 weeks. This is an existential continuity exposure for the product lines dependent on these suppliers.

O-02 CYBER AND IT
Four KRIs are in breach: MTTD at 11 days (breach: 10d), patch compliance at 71% (breach: 80%), IT RTO at 6 hours for OMS (breach: 6h — exactly at threshold), and supplier cyber resilience assessment at 0% (no threshold specified but 0% with 4 active security incidents this month is an explicit escalation trigger).

MTTD of 11 days means a threat actor has, on average, 11 days of undetected access before containment begins. With MTTR at 18 days, total exposure window from intrusion to resolution averages 29 days. In that window, ransomware pre-positioning, data exfiltration, or OMS disruption is entirely feasible. Four security incidents recorded month-to-date indicate this is not a theoretical risk — incidents are occurring now.

Patch compliance at 71% means nearly 3 in 10 systems are running unpatched software. Eight critical vulnerabilities have been open for more than 30 days, and 23 high vulnerabilities similarly. MFA coverage at 87% leaves 13% of access paths without multi-factor protection. Privileged access has not been reviewed in 45 days — a significant governance gap given the active incident environment.

The supplier cyber resilience assessment at 0% is particularly serious in the context of O-01: none of the supply chain partners — including TSMC, Foxconn, and Quanta — have been assessed for cyber posture. A cyber incident at any of these unassessed single-source suppliers could simultaneously trigger supply disruption.

Third-party vendor assessment coverage at 62% means 38% of the broader vendor estate has no cyber posture validation.

Business continuity verdict: OMS RTO at exactly the breach threshold of 6 hours means any OMS outage of material duration will breach SLA and impair order fulfilment. The combination of low patch compliance, 8 open critical vulnerabilities, and 4 active incidents this month places the organisation in an elevated state of cyber exposure with insufficient detection speed to contain fast-moving threats.

O-03 PRODUCT QUALITY
No confirmed safety incidents year-to-date (zero tolerance threshold intact). Field failure rate of 0.019% is well within the 0.05% amber threshold. First-pass yield of 97.2% is healthy. These are genuine positives.

However, three amber signals require attention. Recall readiness score of 72 sits 8 points above the breach threshold (70) and is trending deteriorating — the last recall simulation was conducted 8 months ago, which is a meaningful gap. If a recall were required today, the organisation's preparedness to execute it in a timely, compliant manner is sub-optimal.

Supplier quality rejection rate at 0.68% is in amber (breach: 1.0%), but this aggregate masks a critical outlier: Quanta [SUP005] individually reports a 1.2% rejection rate, which is above the breach threshold. Given Quanta is the sole source for ODM-Laptops and is already flagged for financial distress and low inventory, its above-breach rejection rate means quality problems are emanating from the supply chain's most vulnerable node.

Warranty claims at 1,842 month-to-date represent a direct and ongoing EBITDA cost. While the data does not provide average claim cost, at scale this is a meaningful warranty reserve draw. If Quanta's quality deteriorates further, warranty claims on the laptop line could accelerate.

One near-miss event YTD and zero regulatory product notices are noted — both within acceptable ranges.

Business continuity verdict: Quality risk is not currently threatening continuity, but the combination of a financially distressed sole-source supplier with an above-breach rejection rate, declining recall readiness, and 8 months since last recall simulation creates a scenario where quality failure response could be slower and costlier than necessary.

O-04 TALENT
Three flight-risk employees are confirmed in R&D — triggering the escalation threshold (3 or more in the same critical function). This is the highest-priority talent signal. R&D is the function responsible for product innovation and IP development; the departure of three flagged individuals in this function simultaneously risks project continuity, knowledge transfer gaps, and potential competitive intelligence loss.

Critical open roles beyond 60 days stand at 7 (breach: 6), with Engineering taking 68 days on average to fill critical positions. Engineering attrition is annualised at 14.2% (amber: 12%), sustained by an 8.2% compensation gap versus market. The counter-offer acceptance rate of 42% in Engineering indicates that when employees receive competing offers, fewer than half are being retained — a direct consequence of the compensation gap. IT-Operations attrition at 11.8% is approaching the 12% amber threshold.

SVP succession coverage at 72% (amber: 80%) means nearly 3 in 10 senior leadership roles lack an identified successor. Internal promotion rate at 18% and employee engagement at 67% suggest a workforce that does not strongly identify career growth opportunity within the organisation — a structural driver of attrition rather than a cyclical one.

Mandatory training completion at 91% is adequate. Diversity hire rate at 34% is noted.

Business continuity verdict: The R&D flight-risk cluster is the most immediate concern — departure of these individuals could delay product development cycles with direct revenue consequences. The Engineering talent pipeline, with 7 roles unfilled beyond 60 days and a persistent compensation gap, also slows the organisation's ability to execute on supply chain remediation (dual-sourcing qualification requires engineering resources) and cyber remediation (IT-Operations understaffing links directly to MTTD and patch compliance performance). The talent situation is therefore not isolated — it is a systemic constraint on the organisation's ability to remediate its most critical operational risks.

O-02.name: Cyber attack & IT resilience

cyber_supply_response_gap_days.description: Days by which combined MTTD+MTTR cyber response time exceeds available inventory cover. Positive value = production halt guaranteed if cyber incident disrupts supplier comms. Amber: 0 (response just meets inventory); Breach: >0 (response exceeds inventory). Target: ≤ -2 days (2-day safety margin).

O-03.override_justification: Expert override applied 2026-06-07 (EM-08 panel finding). Arithmetic rating: low (l=2, i=5). Override to: high (l=3, i=5). Justification: Likelihood score of 2 is inconsistent with active breach conditions — warranty_claims_mtd at 1,842 (up from 1,612), recall_readiness at 72 (amber, 2 points from breach), recall simulation last run 8 months ago. Upstream supply quality (Quanta rejection rate 1.2%, Foxconn 0.8%) is a confirmed leading driver of downstream field defects. Per O-04 override precedent: forward-looking indicators and breach trajectory take precedence over lagging control effectiveness score. CRO sign-off required next review cycle.

Forward-looking: 3 senior R&D engineers under active hyperscaler recruitment (confirmed approaches from two major cloud providers); no documented SVP successors for 2 roles; compensation gap widening vs. hyperscaler benchmarks. Override reflects leading indicators not captured in lagging control effectiveness score. CRO sign-off: 2026-06-07 (reissued EM-06 panel correction — prior text erroneously referenced 'senior traders'; corrected to R&D engineering population).

S-01.agent_findings:
# STRATEGIC RISK ASSESSMENT
**Run:** Current Period | **Agent:** Strategic Risk Agent

---

## 1. DOMAIN STATUS
The strategic risk domain is in a **critical posture**: S-01 has breached threshold on geopolitical concentration, S-02 is amber on AI competitive disruption, and S-03 remains within tolerance despite active deal complexity.

---

## 2. S-01 FINDING — GEOPOLITICAL RISK | 🔴 BREACH
Five geopolitical signals are active, of which four are high severity. ## Financial Risk Domain Assessment — 2026-05-28

### F-01: FX & Commodity Risk

**Scope clarification**: Per CF-04 (2026-06-07), F-01 KRI scope is FX-only (Revenue and Cost positions). Commodity positions (COM001 DRAM, COM002 NAND) are cited as supplementary context for EBITDA covenant stress analysis only.

**Unhedged FX Exposure: AMBER — USD 4,080M**
Total unhedged FX exposure of USD 4,080M exceeds the amber threshold of USD 4,000M and triggers the F-01 escalation threshold of USD 500M by a substantial margin. Breach threshold is USD 5,000M (USD 920M of additional deterioration capacity before breach). The four FX positions break down as follows:

- FX001 USD/CNY Revenue: gross USD 3,200M, hedged USD 1,400M (43.8%), unhedged USD 1,800M — largest single unhedged exposure. Hedge ratio below 45% breach threshold. Counterparty: JPMorgan. Maturity: 6 months. Unrealised P&L: USD -8.4M.
- FX002 USD/EUR Revenue: gross USD 2,800M, hedged USD 1,200M (42.9%), unhedged USD 1,600M. Hedge ratio below 45% breach threshold. Counterparty: Deutsche Bank. Maturity: 12 months. Unrealised P&L: +USD 7.2M (only positive P&L position in FX book).
- FX003 USD/JPY Cost: gross USD 980M, hedged USD 600M (61.2%), unhedged USD 380M. Hedge ratio above 60% amber threshold — best-hedged FX position. Counterparty: HSBC. Maturity: 3 months (shortest FX maturity — roll risk imminent). Unrealised P&L: USD -15.6M — largest single FX loss.
- FX004 USD/KRW Cost: gross USD 620M, hedged USD 320M (51.6%), unhedged USD 300M. Hedge ratio within amber band (45%–60%). Counterparty: Citibank. Maturity: 6 months. Unrealised P&L: USD -9.8M.

**Portfolio Average Hedge Ratio: AMBER — 46.3%**
The FX-only portfolio average hedge ratio of 46.3% is within the amber band (45%–60%). Two of four positions (FX001 at 43.8% and FX002 at 42.9%) are individually below the 45% breach threshold. Were either of these positions assessed in isolation, they would be in breach. The blended average is held above 45% by FX003 (61.2%) and FX004 (51.6%).

**FX Unrealised P&L: USD -47.0M**
FX-only unrealised P&L is USD -47.0M. This figure is cited as the F-01 KRI. For covenant stress context: FX unrealised P&L (USD -47.0M) flows through operating income into EBITDA; commodity unrealised P&L (USD -20.4M: COM001 DRAM USD -12.3M via Goldman Sachs, COM002 NAND USD -8.1M via JPMorgan) flows through COGS into gross profit then EBITDA. Total portfolio unrealised loss is USD -67.4M.

**Trend: Deteriorating**. Average hedge ratio of 46.3% is proximate to the 45% breach threshold with two individual positions already below it. FX unrealised P&L is negative across three of four positions. No data supplied indicating an active programme to increase hedge ratios.

**Counterparty concentration note**: JPMorgan appears as counterparty on both FX001 (USD/CNY, gross USD 3,200M) and COM002 (NAND, gross USD 650M). This concentration warrants monitoring but is not a standalone breach under F-01 scope.

---

### F-02: Revenue Concentration & AR Quality

**Top-Customer Concentration: AMBER — 22.1%**
The single largest customer accounts for 22.1% of revenue (Enterprise Tier1 segment), exceeding the 20% amber threshold. The breach threshold is 25.0%, providing 2.9 percentage points of headroom. Government Tier1 is the second-largest segment concentration at 14.2% (within appetite). SMB Tier3 (2.1%) and Consumer Tier4 (1.2%) present negligible single-name concentration risk.

**Bad Debt Provision: BREACH — 1.44%**
The bad debt provision rate of 1.44% is in breach of both the KRI threshold (breach: >0.55%) and, critically, the COV006 Trade-Finance covenant threshold of 0.80%. This is the most acute F-02 finding. Segment analysis of overdue_90d balances identifies the primary drivers:
- SMB Tier3: USD 22M overdue >90 days, provision USD 32.1M (highest provision in absolute terms)
- Enterprise Tier2: USD 18M overdue >90 days, provision USD 28.6M
- Consumer Tier4: USD 12M overdue >90 days, provision USD 18.4M
- Enterprise Tier1: USD 8M overdue >90 days, provision USD 12.4M (lowest overdue rate relative to current balance)
- Government Tier1: USD 0M overdue >90 days, provision USD 2.8M (best-performing segment)

Total bad debt provisions aggregate to USD 94.3M (USD 12.4M + USD 28.6M + USD 32.1M + USD 2.8M + USD 18.4M). This is the full write-off cure cost figure cited in the COV006 analysis.

**Trend: Deteriorating**. Bad debt provision rate has risen from 1.10% (prior test) to 1.44% (current test), deepening the COV006 breach from -0.30pp to -0.64pp below threshold. The concentration risk is flat (no data indicates change in Enterprise Tier1 revenue share).

---

### F-03: Liquidity & Refinancing Risk

**COV006 Bad Debt Provision — CONFIRMED ACTIVE BREACH (Trade-Finance facility)**
Current: 1.44% vs threshold: 0.80%. Headroom: -0.64pp. This is a confirmed breach, not a forward probability. Cure deadline: 2026-06-12. Cross-default risk if notification obligation is not met before the 2026-06-30 test date.

Cure scenarios and COV001 impact:
- Full write-off (USD 94.3M): EBITDA headroom falls from USD 152M to USD ~58M before COV001 breach on RCF-USD2B
- Partial write-off (USD 50M): EBITDA headroom falls from USD 152M to USD ~102M

**BOARD DIRECTIVE REQUIRED — LENDER NOTIFICATION**: Lender notification is a board-authorised act under credit facility terms. Written board authorisation is required before the Group Treasurer contacts lenders. Hard deadline for board resolution: 2026-06-12. Joint owners: Group Treasurer AND CFO (CFO co-signature required per facility terms). Cross-default risk if notification obligation is not discharged before the 2026-06-30 test date.

**COV001 Net Debt/EBITDA — AMBER (RCF-USD2B)**
Current: 2.8x vs threshold: 3.0x. Ratio headroom: 0.2x. EBITDA headroom in dollar terms: USD 152M (derived: Net Debt = 2.8 × USD 2.28B = USD 6.384B; COV001 binding EBITDA floor = USD 6.384B / 3.0x = USD 2.128B; headroom = USD 2.28B − USD 2.128B = USD 152M). Ratio has deteriorated from 2.6x to 2.8x quarter-over-quarter. Next test: 2026-06-30.

Stress scenario — simultaneous crystallisation of total portfolio unrealised losses (USD 67.4M) and full COV006 cure write-off (USD 94.3M) would impose a combined USD 161.7M EBITDA reduction, which exceeds the USD 152M headroom and would cause a COV001 breach. This is a compound risk scenario, not a base case, but the margin is thin and warrants immediate attention.

**COV002 Interest Coverage — OK (Term-Loan-A)**
Current: 4.2x vs threshold: 3.5x. Headroom: 0.7x. Directionally deteriorating (from 1.3x to 0.7x quarter-over-quarter) but within appetite. Next test: 2026-06-30.

**COV003 Liquidity Headroom — OK (RCF-USD2B)**
Current: USD 1.1B vs threshold: USD 0.8B. Headroom: USD 0.3B. Above the F-03 escalation trigger of USD 0.9B. Tightened from USD 1.2B (prior test). Directionally deteriorating. Next test: 2026-06-30.

**COV004 Debt Maturity Runway — AMBER (Bond-2027)**
Current: 8 months vs covenant threshold: 12 months. Below threshold; headroom: 0 months (already in breach of the covenant threshold). The F-03 escalation trigger (below 6 months) is not yet breached. Runway has deteriorated from 11 months to 8 months quarter-over-quarter. At this rate of decay (3 months per quarter), the 6-month escalation trigger would be reached within approximately one quarter. Next test: 2026-12-01.

**COV005 EBITDA — OK (All-Facilities)**
Current: USD 2.28B vs threshold: USD 1.80B. Headroom: USD 0.48B. Declining from USD 2.51B (prior test). Directionally deteriorating. Next test: 2026-06-30.

**Overall F-03 Trend: Deteriorating**. Every measured covenant has compressed quarter-over-quarter. COV006 is in active breach. COV001 headroom is USD 152M with multiple scenarios capable of eroding it to near-zero.

---

### F-04: Accounting & Financial Reporting

**Audit Findings: OK**
The completed AUD003 Internal-SOX Financial Controls audit has returned 1 high finding and 0 critical findings. Open high findings: 1 (amber threshold: ≥2; breach threshold: ≥5). No material weaknesses identified (material weakness count: 0). The F-04 escalation trigger (any material weakness) is not activated.

The audit was completed against a due date of 2026-04-30. The single high finding does not in isolation constitute an escalation event, but it should be tracked to resolution, particularly given the active COV006 breach which may draw lender scrutiny to financial reporting controls.

**Trend: Flat**. One high finding, no material weaknesses, no directional signal from current data.

---

### Cross-Domain Risk Summary

The most acute systemic risk in this reporting period is the COV006 active breach (Trade-Finance, bad debt provision 1.44% vs 0.80%) and its secondary effect on COV001 EBITDA headroom (USD 152M). The compound stress scenario — simultaneous FX loss crystallisation (USD 47.0M FX-only, USD 67.4M total portfolio) and full COV006 cure write-off (USD 94.3M) — would produce a USD 161.7M EBITDA draw against USD 152M headroom, causing a COV001 breach on RCF-USD2B. This is the dominant tail risk and must be managed through coordinated CFO, Group Treasurer, and Board action before 2026-06-12.

### F-01: FX & Commodity Risk

**Scope clarification**: Per CF-04 (2026-06-07), F-01 KRI scope is FX-only (Revenue and Cost positions). Commodity positions (COM001 DRAM, COM002 NAND) are cited as supplementary context for EBITDA covenant stress analysis only.

**Unhedged FX Exposure: AMBER — USD 4,080M**
Total unhedged FX exposure of USD 4,080M exceeds the amber threshold of USD 4,000M and triggers the F-01 escalation threshold of USD 500M by a substantial margin. Breach threshold is USD 5,000M (USD 920M of additional deterioration capacity before breach). The four FX positions break down as follows:

- FX001 USD/CNY Revenue: gross USD 3,200M, hedged USD 1,400M (43.8%), unhedged USD 1,800M — largest single unhedged exposure. Hedge ratio below 45% breach threshold. Counterparty: JPMorgan. Maturity: 6 months. Unrealised P&L: USD -8.4M.
- FX002 USD/EUR Revenue: gross USD 2,800M, hedged USD 1,200M (42.9%), unhedged USD 1,600M. Hedge ratio below 45% breach threshold. Counterparty: Deutsche Bank. Maturity: 12 months. Unrealised P&L: +USD 7.2M (only positive P&L position in FX book).
- FX003 USD/JPY Cost: gross USD 980M, hedged USD 600M (61.2%), unhedged USD 380M. Hedge ratio above 60% amber threshold — best-hedged FX position. Counterparty: HSBC. Maturity: 3 months (shortest FX maturity — roll risk imminent). Unrealised P&L: USD -15.6M — largest single FX loss.
- FX004 USD/KRW Cost: gross USD 620M, hedged USD 320M (51.6%), unhedged USD 300M. Hedge ratio within amber band (45%–60%). Counterparty: Citibank. Maturity: 6 months. Unrealised P&L: USD -9.8M.

**Portfolio Average Hedge Ratio: AMBER — 46.3%**
The FX-only portfolio average hedge ratio of 46.3% is within the amber band (45%–60%). Two of four positions (FX001 at 43.8% and FX002 at 42.9%) are individually below the 45% breach threshold. Were either of these positions assessed in isolation, they would be in breach. The blended average is held above 45% by FX003 (61.2%) and FX004 (51.6%).

**FX Unrealised P&L: USD -47.0M**
FX-only unrealised P&L is USD -47.0M. This figure is cited as the F-01 KRI. For covenant stress context: FX unrealised P&L (USD -47.0M) flows through operating income into EBITDA; commodity unrealised P&L (USD -20.4M: COM001 DRAM USD -12.3M via Goldman Sachs, COM002 NAND USD -8.1M via JPMorgan) flows through COGS into gross profit then EBITDA. Total portfolio unrealised loss is USD -67.4M.

**Trend: Deteriorating**. Average hedge ratio of 46.3% is proximate to the 45% breach threshold with two individual positions already below it. FX unrealised P&L is negative across three of four positions. No data supplied indicating an active programme to increase hedge ratios.

**Counterparty concentration note**: JPMorgan appears as counterparty on both FX001 (USD/CNY, gross USD 3,200M) and COM002 (NAND, gross USD 650M). This concentration warrants monitoring but is not a standalone breach under F-01 scope.

---

### F-02: Revenue Concentration & AR Quality

**Top-Customer Concentration: AMBER — 22.1%**
The single largest customer accounts for 22.1% of revenue (Enterprise Tier1 segment), exceeding the 20% amber threshold. The breach threshold is 25.0%, providing 2.9 percentage points of headroom. Government Tier1 is the second-largest segment concentration at 14.2% (within appetite). SMB Tier3 (2.1%) and Consumer Tier4 (1.2%) present negligible single-name concentration risk.

**Bad Debt Provision: BREACH — 1.44%**
The bad debt provision rate of 1.44% is in breach of both the KRI threshold (breach: >0.55%) and, critically, the COV006 Trade-Finance covenant threshold of 0.80%. This is the most acute F-02 finding. Segment analysis of overdue_90d balances identifies the primary drivers:
- SMB Tier3: USD 22M overdue >90 days, provision USD 32.1M (highest provision in absolute terms)
- Enterprise Tier2: USD 18M overdue >90 days, provision USD 28.6M
- Consumer Tier4: USD 12M overdue >90 days, provision USD 18.4M
- Enterprise Tier1: USD 8M overdue >90 days, provision USD 12.4M (lowest overdue rate relative to current balance)
- Government Tier1: USD 0M overdue >90 days, provision USD 2.8M (best-performing segment)

Total bad debt provisions aggregate to USD 94.3M (USD 12.4M + USD 28.6M + USD 32.1M + USD 2.8M + USD 18.4M). This is the full write-off cure cost figure cited in the COV006 analysis.

**Trend: Deteriorating**. Bad debt provision rate has risen from 1.10% (prior test) to 1.44% (current test), deepening the COV006 breach from -0.30pp to -0.64pp below threshold. The concentration risk is flat (no data indicates change in Enterprise Tier1 revenue share).

---

### F-03: Liquidity & Refinancing Risk

**COV006 Bad Debt Provision — CONFIRMED ACTIVE BREACH (Trade-Finance facility)**
Current: 1.44% vs threshold: 0.80%. Headroom: -0.64pp. This is a confirmed breach, not a forward probability. Cure deadline: 2026-06-12. Cross-default risk if notification obligation is not met before the 2026-06-30 test date.

Cure scenarios and COV001 impact:
- Full write-off (USD 94.3M): EBITDA headroom falls from USD 152M to USD ~58M before COV001 breach on RCF-USD2B
- Partial write-off (USD 50M): EBITDA headroom falls from USD 152M to USD ~102M

Written board authorisation is required before the Group Treasurer contacts lenders. Hard deadline for board resolution: 2026-06-12. Joint owners: Group Treasurer AND CFO (CFO co-signature required per facility terms). Cross-default risk if notification obligation is not discharged before the 2026-06-30 test date.

**COV001 Net Debt/EBITDA — AMBER (RCF-USD2B)**
Current: 2.8x vs threshold: 3.0x. Ratio headroom: 0.2x. EBITDA headroom in dollar terms: USD 152M (derived: Net Debt = 2.8 × USD 2.28B = USD 6.384B; COV001 binding EBITDA floor = USD 6.384B / 3.0x = USD 2.128B; headroom = USD 2.28B − USD 2.128B = USD 152M). Ratio has deteriorated from 2.6x to 2.8x quarter-over-quarter. Next test: 2026-06-30.

Stress scenario — simultaneous crystallisation of total portfolio unrealised losses (USD 67.4M) and full COV006 cure write-off (USD 94.3M) would impose a combined USD 161.7M EBITDA reduction, which exceeds the USD 152M headroom and would cause a COV001 breach. This is a compound risk scenario, not a base case, but the margin is thin and warrants immediate attention.

**COV002 Interest Coverage — OK (Term-Loan-A)**
Current: 4.2x vs threshold: 3.5x. Headroom: 0.7x. Directionally deteriorating (from 1.3x to 0.7x quarter-over-quarter) but within appetite. Next test: 2026-06-30.

**COV003 Liquidity Headroom — OK (RCF-USD2B)**
Current: USD 1.1B vs threshold: USD 0.8B. Headroom: USD 0.3B. Above the F-03 escalation trigger of USD 0.9B. Tightened from USD 1.2B (prior test). Directionally deteriorating. Next test: 2026-06-30.

**COV004 Debt Maturity Runway — AMBER (Bond-2027)**
Current: 8 months vs covenant threshold: 12 months. Below threshold; headroom: 0 months (already in breach of the covenant threshold). The F-03 escalation trigger (below 6 months) is not yet breached. Runway has deteriorated from 11 months to 8 months quarter-over-quarter. At this rate of decay (3 months per quarter), the 6-month escalation trigger would be reached within approximately one quarter. Next test: 2026-12-01.

**COV005 EBITDA — OK (All-Facilities)**
Current: USD 2.28B vs threshold: USD 1.80B. Headroom: USD 0.48B. Declining from USD 2.51B (prior test). Directionally deteriorating. Next test: 2026-06-30.

**Overall F-03 Trend: Deteriorating**. Every measured covenant has compressed quarter-over-quarter. COV006 is in active breach. COV001 headroom is USD 152M with multiple scenarios capable of eroding it to near-zero.

---

### F-04: Accounting & Financial Reporting

**Audit Findings: OK**
The completed AUD003 Internal-SOX Financial Controls audit has returned 1 high finding and 0 critical findings. Open high findings: 1 (amber threshold: ≥2; breach threshold: ≥5). No material weaknesses identified (material weakness count: 0). The F-04 escalation trigger (any material weakness) is not activated.

The audit was completed against a due date of 2026-04-30. The single high finding does not in isolation constitute an escalation event, but it should be tracked to resolution, particularly given the active COV006 breach which may draw lender scrutiny to financial reporting controls.

**Trend: Flat**. One high finding, no material weaknesses, no directional signal from current data.

---

### Cross-Domain Risk Summary

The most acute systemic risk in this reporting period is the COV006 active breach (Trade-Finance, bad debt provision 1.44% vs 0.80%) and its secondary effect on COV001 EBITDA headroom (USD 152M). The compound stress scenario — simultaneous FX loss crystallisation (USD 47.0M FX-only, USD 67.4M total portfolio) and full COV006 cure write-off (USD 94.3M) — would produce a USD 161.7M EBITDA draw against USD 152M headroom, causing a COV001 breach on RCF-USD2B. This is the dominant tail risk and must be managed through coordinated CFO, Group Treasurer, and Board action before 2026-06-12.

C-01.agent_findings:
## Compliance Risk Domain Assessment — 2026-05-28

### DATA RECONCILIATION NOTE
The input data for this run contains two sequential rows for most KRI fields (reflecting two data snapshots, likely prior period and current period). This assessment treats the **second (later) row as the current period value** for KRI status and trend determination, and uses the first row as the prior-period comparator for trend direction. All values cited below are explicitly sourced from the data provided.

---

### C-01: EXPORT CONTROLS & SANCTIONS

**Overall Status: AMBER — No confirmed violations; two structural gaps require prompt action.**

**Export Screening Coverage**
Current export_screening_coverage_pct = **96%** (up from prior period: 95%). The applicable threshold set in the KRI definition is: breach below 95%, amber below 98%. At 96%, the metric sits in the **amber band** and is trending **improving** given the 1-percentage-point rise period-over-period. This does not trigger the zero-tolerance escalation threshold (breach: coverage < 95%), but the 2-percentage-point gap to the 98% amber ceiling means a single operational failure could compress coverage back to breach territory. AUD005 (Export-Control / Trade-Transactions) is currently In-Progress with a due date of 2026-06-30 and zero findings to date; this audit must be maintained on schedule to validate the coverage figure.

NOTE: regulatory_horizon.csv maps DORA (EU, effective 2025-01-17) and NIS2 (EU, effective 2024-10-18) to risk C-01, both with status=Partial and gaps referencing IT incident response plan testing. This agent does NOT assess IT security implications — those gaps are owned by the Operational Agent (O-02 / CISO). They are noted here solely because they appear mapped to C-01 in regulatory_horizon.csv, but no compliance obligation mapping action falls within this agent's authority for those specific gaps.

**Sanctions Violations**
confirmed_sanctions_violations_ytd = **0** in both periods. No zero-tolerance escalation trigger is met. Status: OK, trend: flat.

**Denied Party Matches Pending**
denied_party_matches_pending = **3** (up from prior period: 2). This is an **amber and deteriorating** signal. Three unresolved matches means that three transactions or counterparties remain in a legally ambiguous state. Until these are resolved and cleared or rejected, the organisation cannot assert full screening compliance. The average resolution time has improved to 9 days (from 11 days), which is a positive operational indicator, but the pending count increase is directionally adverse. CCO must ensure all three matches are adjudicated before the export control audit (AUD005) concludes on 2026-06-30.

**Export Licences Expiring Within 30 Days**
export_licence_expiring_30d = **2** (up from prior period: 1). The compliance_metrics.csv data explicitly identifies these as ECC-2024-0047 (TSMC components) and ECC-2024-0061 (DRAM modules), both requiring CCO sign-off before expiry. Failure to renew before the expiry date would constitute unlicensed export activity, which carries strict liability exposure under applicable export control regimes. This is flagged as **amber and deteriorating**. CCO must initiate renewal immediately; given the 9-day average escalation resolution time, there is no margin for delay.

**Transaction Volumes and Flag Rate**
transactions_screened_mtd = 18,420 (up from 17,240). transactions_flagged_mtd = 148 (up from 131). restricted_party_flag_rate_pct = 0.80% (up from 0.76%). The flag rate increase is modest and consistent with the higher transaction volume; it does not by itself indicate deterioration in the risk profile, but it reinforces the need to maintain screening coverage at or above 98% as volumes grow.

---

### C-02: DATA PRIVACY & AI

**Overall Status: BREACH on ai_audit_coverage_pct. No confirmed GDPR or PIPL violations. No formal regulator investigation in audit_log.csv.**

**EU AI Act — Conformity Assessment Coverage (BREACH)**
ai_audit_coverage_pct = **78%** (up from prior period: 74%). KRI threshold: breach below 90%, amber below 95%.

At 78%, this KRI is in **confirmed breach**. The regulatory_horizon.csv records EU-AI-Act (EU, effective 2026-08-02) with status=In-Progress and gap: 'AI audit coverage at 78% vs 100% target.' The mandatory conformity assessment deadline is 2026-08-02 — approximately 66 days from the assessment date of 2026-05-28. With 22 percentage points to close in 66 days, the CTO (owner) must present an accelerated assessment schedule immediately. The trend is **improving** (4-percentage-point gain period-over-period) but the velocity is insufficient: at 4 points per period, two further periods would reach only 86%, still in breach at the statutory deadline.

This is the most acute near-term regulatory compliance risk in the portfolio. Non-compliance with EU AI Act Article conformity assessment requirements by the effective date constitutes a regulatory breach with potential for supervisory action.

**GDPR — DSR Resolution Rate**
gdpr_dsr_resolution_rate_pct = **94%** (up from prior period: 92%). The regulatory_horizon.csv records GDPR (EU, effective 2018-05-25) as status=Compliant in the current period (changed from Partial in prior period), with gap: 'DSR resolution rate 94% vs 95% target.' The KRI definition states this is 'FYI context only, no threshold,' so no KRI status breach is recorded. However, GDPR Article 12(3) requires Data Subject Requests to be resolved within one calendar month. At 94%, 6% of DSRs are not meeting this statutory deadline — this is a legal obligation gap regardless of the internal KRI threshold designation.

AUD004 (Privacy-GDPR / Data-Processing) completed with **2 findings, 1 high**, owned by CPO. The nature of these findings is not described in the audit log data. The high finding requires CCO/CPO to assess whether it constitutes a confirmed GDPR violation. As of the data provided, no confirmed violation is recorded — but the gap between 94% resolution and the 95% internal target (which proxies the GDPR Article 12(3) obligation) must be closed. **If the AUD004 high finding relates to a confirmed failure to resolve DSRs within the statutory period, this would constitute a zero-tolerance escalation trigger. CPO must classify the AUD004 high finding explicitly against this criterion and report back.**

**PIPL (China)**
PIPL status=Compliant. Gap: Annual review due Q3 2026. No current breach. Low urgency; CPO must ensure Q3 2026 review is scheduled.

---

### C-03: ANTI-BRIBERY & ESG

**Overall Status: BREACH on third_party_abac_coverage_pct. No confirmed FCPA or UK Bribery Act violations. Whistleblower high finding does not involve SVP-level or above per data provided.**

**Third-Party ABAC Coverage — FCPA and UK Bribery Act (BREACH)**
third_party_abac_coverage_pct = **89%** (up from prior period: 85%). KRI threshold: breach below 95%, amber below 100%.

At 89%, this KRI is in **confirmed breach**. board_summary:
RISK POSTURE

The organisation records 17 KRI breaches and 18 amber warnings across all four risk domains. With three or more domains simultaneously in breach, the systemic risk flag is active, indicating concurrent cross-domain stress.

STRATEGIC: S-01 has breached threshold on geopolitical concentration, with five geopolitical signals detected; S-02 carries an amber warning on AI competitive disruption. The Chief Executive Officer holds accountability for this domain.

OPERATIONAL: Ten breaches and seven amber warnings make this the most severely stressed domain, spanning supply chain, cyber, and talent sub-domains. Quanta [SUP005] simultaneously presents the lowest supplier financial health score (61), lowest single-source inventory cover (3.9 weeks), and the highest rejection rate (1.2%, breaching the 1.0% threshold) on the ODM-Laptop line. Supplier cyber resilience assessment stands at 0%, meaning none of the eight supply-chain suppliers — including TSMC, Foxconn, and Quanta — have been assessed for cyber posture, creating uncontrolled third-party cyber exposure. Mean time to detect sits at 11 days and patch compliance at 71%, conditions directly linked to IT-Operations attrition of 11.8% annualised and seven critical roles open beyond 60 days. Three R&D employees are flagged as flight risk, connecting potential talent loss to product quality resilience and design-escape probability. Engineering attrition of 14.2% and those same seven unfilled roles impair the organisation's ability to qualify alternative suppliers, directly obstructing remediation of the single-source concentration breach at 62.6% against a 50% threshold. Accountability is distributed across the COO, CISO, and CHRO by sub-domain.

FINANCIAL: Four breaches and seven amber warnings are recorded across FX, concentration, covenant, and credit sub-domains. The FX-currency unrealised loss stands at USD -26.6M; commodity derivative losses (DRAM and NAND) add a further USD -20.4M, producing a combined portfolio unrealised loss of USD -47.0M. Full crystallisation of portfolio losses would reduce COV001 EBITDA headroom from USD 152M to approximately USD 105M before any credit cure. The bad-debt ratio stands at 1.44% against a 0.80% breach threshold, requiring a write-off cure estimated at USD 94.3M in full or USD 50M partially; either scenario further compresses COV001 headroom to approximately USD 58M or USD 102M respectively, against a current reading of 2.8x versus the 3.0x threshold already at amber. Simultaneous crystallisation of total portfolio losses (USD 47.0M) and a full COV006 cure write-off (USD 94.3M) would total USD 141.3M — within the USD 152M headroom but leaving only USD 10.7M of margin on the revolving credit facility covenant. Primary credit risk drivers are Enterprise Tier2 (90-day overdue USD 18M, provision USD 28.6M) and SMB Tier3 (90-day overdue USD 22M, provision USD 32.1M). Bond-2027 maturity runway has shortened from 11 months to 8 months quarter-over-quarter, placing the six-month escalation trigger two months away on current trajectory; any supply-chain disruption impairing EBITDA would further reduce refinancing optionality. Enterprise Tier1 top-customer concentration stands at 22.1% at amber against a 25% breach threshold, and the three active M&A deals — CloudScale Systems, NeuralEdge AI, and DataVault Security — may shift customer revenue concentration and must be assessed against that threshold. The Chief Financial Officer holds accountability for this domain.

COMPLIANCE: Two breaches and two amber warnings are active. Three denied-party matches remain pending resolution while two export licences expire within 30 days; should any pending match confirm a restricted party linked to a Tier-1 or Tier-2 supplier, a simultaneous C-01 sanctions concern and C-03 FCPA/UK Bribery Act third-party due-diligence gap would be triggered. ABAC coverage remains below 100% at 89%, and the CCO must cross-reference denied-party screening results against the active third-party ABAC register. Additionally, privacy audit AUD004 concluded with two findings including one rated high; to the extent any finding implicates personal-data processing systems, the CISO has been flagged for assessment of IT security control adequacy. Accountability rests jointly with the Chief Compliance Officer and General Counsel.

KEY RISK DRIVERS

The most consequential combination of findings this period is the simultaneous breach of financial covenant controls, supply chain concentration, and cyber detection capability — three domains where the failure modes reinforce each other under a single realistic stress scenario. The financial position is the most time-sensitive: one covenant is already confirmed in breach on the Trade Finance facility, with the bad debt provision at 1.44% against a 0.80% threshold, and a board-authorised lender notification is required by 12 June 2026. The EBITDA headroom supporting the revolving credit facility covenant stands at USD 152 million — a margin that would be entirely consumed if unrealised FX and commodity losses of USD 47.0 million were crystallised simultaneously with a full bad debt write-off cure of USD 94.3 million, producing a combined charge of USD 141.3 million and triggering a covenant breach on the revolving credit facility. That stress scenario is not remote: five geopolitical signals are active against a threshold of three, and three of those signals are high severity, meaning the conditions that would crystallise FX losses are present now. The supply chain position compounds this directly — geographic concentration at 75.3% against a 75% threshold, single-source concentration at 62.6% against 50%, and inventory cover at only 3.9 weeks against a 4-week threshold mean that a disruption in Taiwan or China would simultaneously impair revenue generation and reduce the EBITDA available to service the debt structure with a debt maturity runway of only 8 months. Against this backdrop, the cyber domain presents a further vulnerability: with 11 days on average to detect a threat and patch compliance at 71%, the organisation's ability to prevent a cyber-driven operational or revenue disruption is materially below policy standards. The CFO, COO, and CISO bear the primary accountability for the three most consequential breach clusters this period.

CROSS-DOMAIN CONNECTIONS

The most material cross-domain interaction this period runs from talent through cyber and into financial resilience. The IT-Operations function is understaffed — seven critical roles have been open beyond 60 days, the talent breach triggered by the critical_open_roles_gt60d KRI — and this staffing gap directly contributes to the 11-day mean time to detect threats, which is itself in breach. With mean time to detect at 11 days and mean time to recover at 18 days, the total window from intrusion to resolution averages 29 days. Staff in revenue-critical functions, including three flagged R&D flight risks, are at elevated risk of departure, and R&D departures create a direct pathway to product quality escapes and delayed supplier qualification. If a cyber incident occurred during an active talent gap — a condition that exists today — the organisation's capacity to contain, respond, and recover would be operating below its own minimum standards, and any resulting revenue or operational disruption would flow directly into the EBITDA headroom that is already within USD 152 million of the revolving credit facility covenant. A second important interaction runs from supply chain into financial and compliance domains: none of the organisation's eight supply chain partners have been assessed for cyber resilience, meaning a cyber incident at a sole-source supplier such as TSMC or Foxconn would simultaneously constitute an uncontrolled third-party cyber event and a supply disruption capable of impairing EBITDA generation and undermining the debt refinancing capacity needed before the Bond-2027 maturity. The compliance dimension adds a further layer: three pending denied-party matches remain unresolved alongside two export licences expiring within 30 days, and if any match resolves to a restricted party associated with a Tier-1 or Tier-2 supplier, it would simultaneously create a sanctions exposure under C-01 and an anti-bribery due diligence gap under C-03 — potentially implicating the same suppliers already identified as critical supply chain single points of failure. The Quanta supplier node crystallises several of these interactions in one entity: it is simultaneously the lowest-health sole-source supplier with a financial health score of 61, the supplier with the lowest inventory buffer at 3.9 weeks, and the supplier with the highest quality rejection rate at 1.2% against a 1.0% breach threshold, meaning a single failure at that node would impair supply continuity, product quality, and EBITDA concurrently.

QUARTER-ON-QUARTER MOVEMENT

Aggregate residual risk has risen materially from the prior period, driven principally by inherent risk growth rather than control weakening — meaning the underlying exposures and external threat environment have intensified, not merely that the organisation has failed to maintain its defences, though there are elements of both. The dominant cause is inherent risk growth. Geopolitical signals moved from two to five, crossing from amber into breach, reflecting a genuine deterioration in the external environment rather than any internal control failure. Inventory cover at Quanta moved from 4.3 weeks to 3.9 weeks, crossing into breach, as the supplier's operational condition worsened. The bad debt provision rate continued its deteriorating trajectory, remaining in breach and deepening, as credit quality in the SMB and Enterprise Tier 2 customer segments declined. The average hedge ratio moved from 50.3% to 46.3%, remaining in amber but drawing closer to the 45% breach threshold, as underlying FX exposures grew without a commensurate increase in hedging activity — an element of control weakening within an otherwise externally driven picture. Four KRIs crossed from a better status into breach this period: mean time to detect threats, critical open roles beyond 60 days, inventory cover, and geopolitical signal count. Recall readiness deteriorated from ok to amber, declining from 82% to 72%, approaching the 70% breach threshold. Competitive signals moved from ok to amber as the AI-native product landscape shifted. Partially offsetting these movements, synergy delivery on the M&A programme improved from 65% to 84%, moving from amber to ok, and both AI audit coverage and third-party ABAC coverage improved modestly, though both remain in breach. The single KRI whose reversal would most reduce residual risk this period is mean time to detect threats, currently at 11 days against the 10-day threshold. Reducing detection time is the primary lever because it directly gates the organisation's ability to contain the cyber incidents that are already occurring — four were recorded in the current month — and because the degraded detection capability is itself the product of the talent gap that also slows supply chain remediation and quality response. Addressing the MTTD breach through the workforce plan and remediation roadmap already directed to the CISO and CHRO is the board's highest-leverage single decision point this period.

RISK COMMITTEE RECOMMENDED ACTIONS:
  • Direct the CFO to brief the Committee at this meeting on the active covenant breach, cross-default risk status, and the lender engagement plan — noting that simultaneous crystallisation of the USD 47.0 million total unrealised FX and commodity losses and the full bad debt cure write-off of USD 94.3 million would produce a combined charge of USD 141.3 million against EBITDA headroom of USD 152 million, triggering a covenant breach on the revolving credit facility. Require the CFO to present a written remediation plan within 14 days covering bad debt cure sequencing, hedging adequacy, and covenant waiver or amendment strategy.
  • Direct the Chief Operating Officer and VP Supply Chain to present at this meeting an emergency plan to address the five concurrent supply chain breaches — single-source concentration at 62.6%, geographic concentration at 75.3%, inventory cover at 3.9 weeks, zero supplier cyber resilience assessments completed, and Quanta's simultaneous financial distress, low inventory buffer, and rejection rate breach on the ODM-Laptop line. Require the COO to table a supplier diversification roadmap, an inventory build target, and a supplier cyber resilience assessment schedule, noting that engineering attrition and 7 open critical roles are directly impeding dual-sourcing execution.
  • Require the CISO to deliver to the Committee within 30 days a time-bound remediation plan addressing all four concurrent cyber control breaches — mean time to detect at 11 days, patch compliance at 71%, MFA coverage at 87%, and privileged access reviews at 45 days outstanding — alongside a workforce plan coordinated with the CHRO to fill the 7 critical IT roles open beyond 60 days. Approve interim escalation of privileged access review and patch deployment as emergency measures given the direct link between understaffing and degraded threat detection capability.
  • Direct the Chief Compliance Officer and General Counsel to report to the Committee on the resolution status of the three pending denied-party matches, given that unresolved matches combined with two export licences expiring within 30 days and third-party ABAC coverage at 89% against a 95% threshold create the risk of a simultaneous sanctions and anti-bribery compliance gap. Require the CCO to confirm that denied-party screening results are being cross-referenced against the active ABAC third-party register and to set a resolution deadline for all pending matches.
  • Direct the CEO to brief the Committee on the strategic response to five geopolitical signals breaching the threshold of three, noting the combined pressure this places on unhedged FX exposure of USD 4,080 million, supply chain geographic concentration at 75.3%, and the debt maturity runway of 8 months which is 2 months from its escalation trigger. Require the CEO to present scenario-based contingency options for the Committee's consideration, and direct the CFO and Group Treasurer to assess whether incremental FX hedging cover is warranted given the elevated geopolitical signal count.

cov001_board_narrative.text: IMPORTANT — COV001 AMBER RATIO VS FORWARD BREACH PROBABILITY: The Net Debt to EBITDA ratio currently reads 2.8× (amber — below the 3.0× breach ceiling). This ratio reflects the position as at the Q2 reporting date and is correct. However, the EBITDA Stress Monte Carlo separately estimates a 69.4% probability that this ratio will exceed 3.0× by the 2026-06-30 test date — a breach-status forward indicator. These two signals are not contradictory: the ratio measures where the organisation is today; the Monte Carlo measures where it is likely to be in 23 days under plausible EBITDA scenarios. Both signals must be read together. The amber ratio does NOT indicate the covenant position is under control — the forward probability of 69.4% means there is a greater-than-two-thirds chance of a COV001 breach at the next test date. The board should treat this as a breach-trajectory situation requiring the same urgency as an active breach. A deterministic scenario in which the COV006 provision charge of approximately USD 365M is recognised in full would reduce EBITDA to approximately USD 1,915M — below the COV001 implied floor of USD 2,128M — giving P(breach) = 100% at test date.
